title: New Era Custom Caps: Pro-Sports Style for Your Logo
metaDescription: Custom New Era caps with your logo — fitted, snapback, adjustable & knit beanies. Premium sports-heritage headwear from NWCA in Milton, WA. Get a quote.
bodyMarkdown:
Some caps look like a giveaway. Others look like a brand. If you want your logo on headwear that reads current, athletic, and premium — the kind of cap people actually keep wearing — [New Era](/custom-new-era) is the name that gets you there. It's the on-field baseball brand, so it carries real pro-sports and streetwear cachet the moment someone puts it on. For crews, breweries, gyms, and companies here in the Pacific Northwest who want their logo to look like a lifestyle label instead of a handout, this is the headwear line to build around.

## Why New Era reads premium

A structured New Era cap sits differently than a soft, unstructured hat. The crowns hold their shape and the fits feel dialed-in — the same silhouettes you see on dugouts and in streetwear drops. That heritage does the heavy lifting for your brand. When your logo lands on a cap people already associate with pro sports, it borrows that credibility automatically.

New Era also spans the full range of how people like to wear a hat: a snug modern stretch fit, a classic adjustable, a snapback, and a cold-weather knit beanie. That means one brand can outfit an entire team — the crew lead who wants a fitted look, the front-office folks who want adjustable, and everybody who needs a beanie for a January jobsite in Milton.

## The New Era styles we recommend

- **[NE1000](/product.html?style=NE1000)** — Structured stretch fit with a clean, modern sports look. This is the flagship for logo embroidery when you want that premium, athletic feel. Great front-panel canvas for a bold mark.
- **NE400** — The adjustable option. Easy one-size-fits-most fit with the same New Era polish, so it's the safe pick when you're ordering for a mixed group and don't want to chase individual sizes.
- **NE200 / NE205** — Snapbacks and a streetwear favorite. If your brand skews younger, trend-forward, or event-driven, this is the cap that gets worn off the clock too.
- **NE1020** — The knit beanie. Pacific Northwest weather makes this a year-round seller, and a tidy embroidered or patch logo on the cuff looks sharp all season.

Between these four, you can cover fitted, adjustable, snapback, and beanie without ever leaving the New Era family — which keeps your whole lineup looking like it belongs together.

## Getting your logo on it right

New Era caps take decoration well, and the structured crowns give embroidery a firm, clean surface to sit on. A raised embroidered logo looks especially good on the NE1000 and NE400. For the snapbacks, many brands like the look of a woven or leather patch on the front panel — it leans into that streetwear vibe the NE200/NE205 is known for. On the NE1020 beanie, a compact embroidered mark on the cuff keeps things clean.

We handle all of it in-house at our Milton, WA shop, so we can talk through stitch count, logo placement, and whether embroidery or a patch will make your mark pop before anything goes on a cap. If you're outfitting a team, ordering for an event, or building retail-ready merch, we'll help you match the right New Era style to the look you're after.

## Ready to build your caps?

Pick the silhouette that fits your brand, and we'll take care of the rest. Start on the [New Era brand page](/custom-new-era) to see the lineup, or jump straight to the [NE1000](/product.html?style=NE1000) if you already know you want that premium fitted look. When you're ready to lock in colors, logo placement, and decoration, [request a quote](/pages/request-a-quote.html) and our team will get you set up. Give your logo the pro-sports treatment it deserves.

title: Custom OGIO Bags & Polos: Corporate Gifts People Keep
metaDescription: Custom OGIO backpacks, tech bags, and performance polos from NWCA in Milton, WA — premium corporate gifts, onboarding kits, and client swag. Get a quote.
bodyMarkdown:
Most corporate swag ends up in a drawer. OGIO gear ends up on someone's shoulder at the airport. That's the whole difference, and it's why OGIO is our go-to brand when a Puget Sound company wants a gift that actually gets used — a backpack the recipient carries to work every day, a travel bag that survives a real trip, a polo sharp enough to wear on the floor at a trade show. For onboarding kits, client thank-yous, and event swag, OGIO punches above its weight because it looks and feels like something a person would buy for themselves.

We decorate OGIO here in Milton, WA — in-house embroidery for that clean, dimensional logo on a bag panel or a polo chest. Below is how we pair the pieces for the three jobs corporate buyers ask us about most.

## OGIO Bags: The Gift That Doesn't Get Tossed

Bags are the anchor of any premium gift program. They're big enough to feel substantial, useful enough to keep, and the flat panels take an embroidered logo beautifully. For remote-team onboarding kits or a top-tier client gift, a well-chosen OGIO bag does more brand work over a year than a stack of pens ever will.

- **[OGIO 411092](/product.html?style=411092)** — a laptop-friendly backpack with structured, padded compartments; the workhorse of the lineup and our most-requested onboarding-kit anchor.
- **OGIO 411073** — a tech-forward pack built for the commute-and-travel crowd; smart organization for cables, chargers, and a laptop, so it reads as a real gift, not a giveaway.

Both give you a generous embroidery zone for a company mark, and both are the kind of thing new hires actually show up with on day one. Browse the full range on our [OGIO brand page](/custom-ogio).

## OGIO Performance Polos: Sharp for Staff and Events

When you need your team to look put-together at a conference booth, a client site, or a company event, OGIO's performance polos hit the mark. Moisture-wicking, wrinkle-resistant, and cut to look tailored rather than boxy — they photograph well and hold up through a long day on your feet. A left-chest embroidered logo turns them into instant uniform.

- **OG101** — the flagship men's performance polo; clean drape, athletic feel, and a smooth surface that embroiders crisply.
- **OG152** — a step up in texture and detail for teams that want a slightly more premium, modern look at events.
- **LOG101** — the women's cut of the flagship polo, so your whole crew matches in the right fit rather than a unisex compromise.

Pair the polos with a bag and you've got a coordinated onboarding kit or event set that looks intentional — the kind of thing that makes a small company look established and an established one look dialed-in.

## Build a Kit That Feels Considered

The reason OGIO works so well for gifting is that the pieces stack. A backpack plus a matching polo, embroidered with the same logo, reads as a designed set rather than a pile of freebies. Here's how we see companies around the Pacific Northwest putting it together:

- **New-hire onboarding** — a 411092 backpack loaded with an OG101 or LOG101 polo; the new employee arrives their first week already carrying the brand.
- **Client and partner gifts** — a standalone 411073 tech bag; premium enough to say thank-you without a card that reads "promotional."
- **Event and trade-show crews** — matching OG101 / OG152 / LOG101 polos so the whole booth looks like one team.

Because we run embroidery in-house, we control the logo quality and the timeline — important when you've got an onboarding date or a show deadline that won't move.

## Ready to Spec Your OGIO Program?

Tell us the pieces, the quantity, and your logo, and we'll put together a quote. Start on our [OGIO brand page](/custom-ogio) to see the lineup, or jump straight to [requesting a quote](/pages/request-a-quote.html) and we'll help you build a gift or onboarding kit your team and clients will actually hold onto. Local to Milton or shipping across the country, we've got you covered.

title: Best Affordable Custom Tees: Gildan vs District vs Port & Company
metaDescription: Compare Gildan 2000, District DT6000, and Port & Company PC54/PC61 custom tees on softness, budget, and best use. Get a quote from our Milton, WA team.
bodyMarkdown:
Not every custom tee needs to be the same tee. A giveaway you're printing hundreds of for a street fair has a different job than a retail-feel merch shirt you want people to actually wear, which is different again from the plain everyday staple your crew reorders every quarter. The good news: three well-known lines cover those three jobs cleanly, and all of them keep your per-shirt cost sensible. Here's how we at Northwest Custom Apparel steer Milton and greater Puget Sound clients toward the right one.

## The three lines at a glance

Think of it as budget bulk, soft retail feel, and core value. Each brand earned its spot for a reason, and picking by use-case beats chasing the "best" shirt in the abstract.

- **[Gildan 2000 (Ultra Cotton)](/product.html?style=2000)** — The budget bulk workhorse. A sturdy, heavier cotton feel that holds a print well and survives the wash cycle. This is the shirt for big giveaways, fun runs, church events, and anywhere quantity and durability matter more than a fashion drape.
- **[District DT6000 (Very Important Tee)](/product.html?style=DT6000)** — The soft, retail-feel pick. A lighter, smoother hand that reads like something off a boutique rack. Reach for it when the shirt itself is the product: band merch, brewery and coffee-shop swag, and fashion-forward company gear people choose to wear out.
- **Port & Company PC54 / PC61** — The core value staple. A dependable mid-weight cotton (the PC61 runs a touch heavier and more structured than the PC54) that nails everyday branding — uniforms, volunteer shirts, and steady reorders where you want consistency you can count on.

## Match the tee to the job

**Big-budget giveaway → Gildan 2000.** When you're ordering by the hundreds and the cost per shirt multiplies fast, Ultra Cotton is the honest answer. It's roomy, it takes a bold screen print cleanly, and nobody expects a boutique drape from a free event tee — they expect it to look sharp and last. Browse the full lineup on our [Gildan page](/custom-gildan).

**Soft retail merch → District DT6000.** If someone is going to pull your shirt on because they *like* it — not because they got it free — softness wins. The Very Important Tee has that broken-in feel out of the box, so your logo lives on a shirt people keep in rotation. That's what turns merch into walking advertising. See more on our [District page](/custom-district).

**Everyday value branding → Port & Company PC54 / PC61.** For the shirts you'll reorder again and again — front-desk staff, warehouse crews, community groups — you want a reliable middle. The PC54 is the classic go-to; step up to the PC61 when you want a bit more heft and structure. Both take embroidery and print well and keep the whole program affordable. Details on our [Port & Company page](/custom-port-and-company).

## A few practical notes from the shop floor

Softness and weight are a trade-off, not a ranking. A heavier cotton like the Gildan 2000 feels substantial and rugged; a lighter, softer tee like the DT6000 drapes better but reads more casual-fashion than heavy-duty. Neither is "better" — they're built for different rooms.

Color range and sizing matter too. All three lines carry a deep run of colors and a full size range, so matching team colors or covering a broad crew usually isn't a problem. If you're printing one bold logo, a screen print on any of these looks great; if you want a soft-hand, photo-real graphic, ask us about the options — we run embroidery, screen print, DTG, and DTF in-house right here in Milton, so we can point you to the decoration that fits the shirt and the look.

When in doubt, order a couple of blanks and feel them yourself before committing to a big run. We'd rather you love the shirt in hand than guess from a screen.

## Ready to price it out?

Tell us the quantity, the shirt, and the artwork, and we'll get you a real number fast. Start a [screen print quote](/quote-builders/screenprint-quote-builder.html) or [request a quote](/pages/request-a-quote.html) and we'll help you land on the right tee for your event, your merch table, or your everyday crew. Family-owned in Milton, WA since 1977 — we'll make sure the shirt fits the job.

title: Premium Branded Jackets: Eddie Bauer & The North Face Gifts
metaDescription: Custom Eddie Bauer & The North Face soft shells, fleece, and vests for executive gifts and client-facing outerwear. Premium branded jackets. Get a quote.
bodyMarkdown:
There's a difference between a giveaway and a gift. A giveaway gets worn once and ends up in the garage. A gift gets worn to the airport, to the job site, to the kid's soccer game on a cold Saturday. When you're thanking a top client, recognizing a leadership team, or handing out something at a milestone event, the label on the jacket does a lot of the talking. That's where [Eddie Bauer](/custom-eddie-bauer) and [The North Face](/custom-north-face) earn their place.

Both are names people already trust and already own. Put your logo on one and you're not asking someone to advertise for you — you're giving them outerwear they'd have bought anyway. Here in the Pacific Northwest, where a soft shell or fleece is basically a year-round uniform, that logic lands especially well. At our shop in Milton, WA, we've stitched these brands for executive gifts, client appreciation runs, and small leadership orders that need to look considered, not corporate.

## Soft Shells That Read as Executive

The soft shell is the workhorse of the premium gift category — wind-resistant, water-repellent, and clean enough to layer over a collared shirt without looking like field gear. It's the piece that travels well and photographs well, which matters when your logo is going to be seen in meetings and on the road.

- **[Eddie Bauer EB532 Soft Shell Jacket](/product.html?style=EB532)** — Our go-to for executive and client gifts. Structured, wind- and water-resistant, and the fabric takes an embroidered logo cleanly. Looks like something someone chose for themselves.
- **[The North Face soft shells](/custom-north-face)** — A recognizable, weather-ready line for teams who want the brand's outdoor credibility on a client-facing piece. A strong choice when recipients are outdoorsy or field-based.

Embroidery is almost always the right decoration here. A tastefully sized left-chest logo keeps the garment feeling like a gift rather than a billboard — restraint is the whole point at this tier.

## Fleece and Vests for the Layered Look

Not every gift needs to be a full jacket. Fleece and vests hit a lower-key, more versatile note — the kind of thing people reach for on a cool morning and keep reaching for. They're also a smart way to outfit a leadership group in something coordinated without it reading as a uniform.

- **[Eddie Bauer fleece](/custom-eddie-bauer)** — Soft, warm, and endlessly wearable. A safe, well-liked pick for broad recipient lists where you want everyone happy.
- **[Eddie Bauer vests](/custom-eddie-bauer)** — The vest is the quiet MVP of corporate gifting: layers over anything, flatters every body type, and gets worn three seasons a year.
- **[The North Face fleece](/custom-north-face)** — Brings the brand's name recognition to a cozier, casual-forward piece. Great for client gifts where the recipient skews active or outdoorsy.

Fleece and vests embroider beautifully and suit a subtle tonal logo if you want something even more understated — think a matching-thread mark that shows up close but never shouts.

## Picking the Right Piece for the Right Recipient

A few things we tell clients when they're building a premium gift order:

- **Match the brand to the person.** Eddie Bauer reads polished and classic; The North Face reads active and adventurous. Both are premium — pick the one your recipient already gravitates toward.
- **Order a range of sizes early.** Premium outerwear moves, and you want the full run available before a gifting deadline. Tell us your timeline and we'll plan around it.
- **Keep the logo restrained.** At this tier, smaller and cleaner wins. A left-chest embroidered mark almost always looks more expensive than anything large.
- **Consider a mixed order.** A soft shell for the executive team, vests for the wider group — same logo, coordinated look, handled in one order.

Because our pricing is quote-based, you'll get numbers built around your exact styles, quantities, and decoration — not a shelf sticker. That's how we make sure a small leadership gift and a larger client run each get the right treatment.

## Ready to Build Your Gift Order?

If you're planning executive gifts, client appreciation, or a leadership refresh, start with the styles above and let us tailor the details. Browse the full [Eddie Bauer](/custom-eddie-bauer) and [The North Face](/custom-north-face) lineups, then [request a quote](/pages/request-a-quote.html) with your styles and quantities. Tell us who it's for and your timeline — we'll help you land on outerwear people actually keep.

title: Bella+Canvas 3001: The Soft Retail Tee for Merch & Brands
metaDescription: Custom Bella+Canvas 3001 tees for merch drops, bands & brands. Soft, fashion-fit, huge color range, prints great. Get a quote from NWCA in Milton, WA.
bodyMarkdown:
When a band, boutique, or brand wants merch that people actually keep and wear, they reach for the [Bella+Canvas 3001](/product.html?style=BC3001). It's the tee that turned "band shirt" into "everyday shirt" — soft, fitted, and cut like something you'd buy off a retail rack, not a giveaway pile. For crews and companies across Milton and the Pacific Northwest who care how their merch feels on, this is the go-to.

We print the 3001 family in-house every week, and it's earned its reputation. The fabric takes ink beautifully, the fit flatters a wide range of bodies, and the color range is deep enough to match almost any brand palette. If your logo or design deserves a shirt people wear on purpose, start here.

## Why the 3001 Owns the Merch Rack

The 3001 is a retail-fit tee, which means it's trimmer through the body and shoulders than a boxy workwear staple. That's exactly what merch buyers want — a shirt that looks intentional, holds its shape, and reads as a real product. The ringspun cotton is smooth and lightweight, so screen print ink sits crisp and DTG prints land with sharp detail and soft hand.

It also comes in one of the widest color libraries in the business. That matters when you're matching a brand's exact vibe — a boutique's signature dusty rose, a band's blackout tour look, a coffee roaster's earthy neutrals. You're not forced to compromise your palette to fit a blank.

## The 3001 Family, Style by Style

Pick the cut that fits the drop. Every one of these prints the same clean way, so you can build a whole line that feels cohesive:

- **[BC3001](/product.html?style=BC3001)** — the unisex soft-style tee and the modern retail standard. Your default for any merch drop, band shirt, or brand tee.
- **BC3001CVC** — the same beloved fit in heather CVC colors, for that lived-in, vintage-soft look that boutiques and lifestyle brands love.
- **BC3005** — the V-neck version, a little more relaxed and fashion-forward for apparel lines that want an alternative to the crew.
- **BC3501** — the long sleeve, perfect for cooler PNW months, layered merch, and drops that want a premium feel.
- **BC3719** — the pullover hoodie in the same soft family, so your tee and your hoodie actually match across the whole collection.

Building a range across these styles gives your customers options across seasons and styles while keeping one consistent, high-quality look. That's how the strongest merch lines are built.

## Print Method: DTG or Screen Print

The 3001 is a printer's favorite because it plays well with both of our high-volume methods. For full-color art, photo-real designs, or small runs where you don't want to commit to a big order, DTG lays ink directly into that smooth cotton with excellent detail. For bold, punchy designs and larger quantities, screen print delivers rich, durable color that holds up wash after wash.

Not sure which way to go? Tell us the design and the quantity and we'll steer you to the right method. A simple two-color band logo on 100 tees and a full-color boutique graphic on a small run are two different jobs, and we'll match the process to your art.

Embroidery is also on the table if you want a subtle left-chest logo look, though most merch and brand work on the 3001 leans toward print for that big-graphic impact.

## Get Your Merch Started

Whether you're a band prepping for a tour, a boutique launching a house line, or a brand planning a drop, the Bella+Canvas 3001 family is the soft, retail-quality foundation to build on. Browse the full lineup on our [Bella+Canvas brand page](/custom-bella-canvas), then send us your design and quantities.

Ready to price it out? Head to our [screen print and DTG quote builder](/quote-builders/screenprint-quote-builder.html) or [request a quote](/pages/request-a-quote.html) and we'll get your merch moving. We've been decorating apparel in Milton, WA since 1977 — let's make something your customers actually want to wear.

title: TravisMathew: Premium Polos & Quarter-Zips for Client-Facing Teams
metaDescription: Custom TravisMathew polos and quarter-zips for company golf, executives, and client-facing staff. Premium brand-name apparel decorated in Milton, WA.
bodyMarkdown:
There's a moment when a standard uniform polo stops being good enough. Maybe it's the annual client golf outing, a leadership retreat, or the sales team walking into a customer's boardroom. When the people wearing your logo represent the top of your brand, the shirt should match. That's exactly where [TravisMathew](/custom-travismathew) earns its place in a company apparel program — premium, recognizable, and comfortable enough that people actually want to keep wearing it off the clock.

TravisMathew built its name on golf apparel that doesn't look like golf apparel. The fabrics move, the fits are modern, and the finish reads upscale rather than corporate. For Pacific Northwest crews and companies here around Milton and the greater Puget Sound, it's become our go-to when a client wants something a clear step above the mid-tier polos most teams start with.

## Why TravisMathew for executives and client-facing staff

The value of a name brand on your team is simple: recognition. When a customer sees a familiar premium label, it signals that your company invested in the people wearing it. TravisMathew delivers that without pushing into stiff, formal territory. The pieces feel like weekend clothes that happen to carry your logo, which is precisely why people wear them long after the event ends — extending your brand's reach every time.

These garments take embroidery beautifully. A clean left-chest logo on a TravisMathew polo looks intentional and premium, not slapped on. For golf tournaments, executive gifts, and client-facing sales teams, that quality difference is the whole point.

## The styles worth featuring

- **[TM1MU411](/product.html?style=TM1MU411)** — A signature premium polo with a modern fit and a soft, performance-minded feel. A strong flagship choice for executives and client meetings.
- **TM1MU414** — Another premium polo option, giving you a styling or color alternative while keeping the same upscale TravisMathew look across the team.
- **TM1MY402** — A women's polo cut for a flattering fit, so mixed teams look coordinated without settling for a unisex compromise.
- **TM1MW451** — A layering piece that works as a polished layer over a polo or on its own for cooler Northwest mornings on the course and in client settings.

Mixing these gives you a complete, cohesive look: premium polos for the core team, the women's cut so everyone fits right, and a layering piece that pulls it together when the weather turns — which, around here, it reliably does.

## Built for company golf and beyond

If a golf event is what's driving this, TravisMathew is a natural fit — it's the kind of apparel the brand is known for. Pair the polos and layers with the rest of a coordinated event package and you've got a program that looks tournament-ready from the first tee to the awards dinner. Our [golf tournament apparel](/golf-tournament-apparel) program is set up to handle exactly this: matching decorated pieces for players, staff, and sponsors, all pulled together on one order.

Beyond the course, the same pieces carry over to trade shows, executive travel, client visits, and premium employee gifts. That versatility is what makes TravisMathew a smart choice — you're not buying event apparel that gets worn once, you're buying a wardrobe your team reaches for.

## Bring your logo to the premium tier

If your standard polo program has been getting the job done but you want a look that matches your best clients and biggest moments, TravisMathew is the upgrade. We'll help you pick the right styles and colors, decorate them in-house here in Milton, and get everyone outfitted in time for your event.

[Request a quote](/pages/request-a-quote.html) and let us know what you're outfitting — a golf outing, an executive team, or a client-facing crew — and we'll put together the right TravisMathew package for it.
